top_issues.title: Diesel injector failure and fuel-in-oil contamination
top_issues.description: Diesel variants (1.4 TDCI and 1.6 TDCI) are prone to injector failure, which can occur from around 60,000 miles onwards. Faulty injectors or injector seals allow fuel to contaminate the engine oil, triggering warning lights and loss of power. Replacement of all four injectors typically costs £1,200, and the problem is common enough that it appears repeatedly in owner reports.
typical_symptoms: Loss of power or engine hesitation when warm
Engine entering limp-home mode with cog and exclamation mark warning light
Rough idle or stuttering at motorway speeds

top_issues.title: Petrol engine spark plug failure causing catastrophic engine damage
top_issues.description: Petrol engines (particularly 1.25-litre variants) can suffer spark plug failure, which in some cases has destroyed the engine entirely. The issue appears linked to owners missing the scheduled spark plug replacement at the third service (37,500 miles). A single failed spark plug can cause an exhaust valve to burn out, resulting in engine damage that costs over £1,200 to repair.
typical_symptoms: Engine warning light or no warning before failure

top_issues.title: Front suspension arm snapping and rear subframe bush deterioration
top_issues.description: The front lower suspension arms can snap unexpectedly, as reported on a 2010 model at just 32,000 miles. Additionally, from around 50,000 to 60,000 miles, the rear subframe rubber bushes deteriorate, leading to MoT advisories. Both issues affect the car's structural integrity and handling safety.
typical_symptoms: Clunking or knocking from the front suspension
Pulling to one side when braking or accelerating
MoT advisory for subframe bush wear

top_issues.title: Power steering clunkiness and CV joint bearing wear
top_issues.description: Many owners report notchy or clunky power steering, particularly between 10 and 30 mph. The problem is caused by dry bearings in the upper CV joint beneath the electric power steering motor, which lose lubrication due to heat. Whilst the issue can be resolved by removing and re-greasing the joint, it requires specialist knowledge and is not a simple dealer fix.
typical_symptoms: Notchy or clunky steering feel at low speeds
Creaking or knocking from the steering column

top_issues.title: Automatic transmission failures (Powershift and torque converter)
top_issues.description: Both the Powershift dry-clutch automatic and the 4-speed torque converter automatic can fail prematurely. Powershift units suffer from dry clutch pack failure, whilst torque converter automatics lose power and require engine restart to recover. Repairs are costly and the problem has been widespread enough to trigger a consumer protection order in Thailand.
typical_symptoms: Need to stop, switch off engine, and restart to regain power
Judder or hesitation when pulling away

overall_reliability_note: The Fiesta is generally well-built with a good reputation, but diesel variants require careful attention to injector condition and fuel quality, petrol engines demand timely spark plug replacement, and automatic transmissions can be costly to repair.
